apps.name: Property Manager V1
apps.summary: Streamlined maintenance command center for small landlords: request intake, status tracking, vendor dispatch, tenant updates, and issue history.
apps.reviewGate: Jeff cleared auth/authz as PASS WITH CONDITIONS for a controlled private beta; broader QA and hardening are still required before anything beyond that.
apps.nextStep: Execution has started on the sellability path. PostgreSQL/deployment planning is now documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_POSTGRES_DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md (commit 5493a36), operator org-scoping hardening landed in commit 8cb7e16, onboarding direction is formalized in PROPERTY_MANAGER_ONBOARDING_INVITE_SPEC.md, and the implementation path is broken down in PROPERTY_MANAGER_INVITE_SYSTEM_BUILD_PLAN.md. Product direction now also includes optional region support, explicit urgency/expected-completion workflow controls, and CSV import/export with full history retention plus a default last-12-month reporting lens. Next: stabilize the auth boundary test harness, have Jeff verify the new org-scope protections, finish invite-system hardening, then add the region layer and carry urgency/expected-completion/import-export capabilities cleanly through operator, vendor, and tenant flows.
notes: Barry Bot: Sim approved Property Manager V1 for complete build.
Barry Bot: Jeff is the explicit post-build test gate for this app.
Barry Bot: promoted to one of the two clearest active app tracks.
Barry Bot: converted the concept into a concrete MVP spec and build checklist so Elon has an executable package.
Barry Bot: initial property-manager scaffold was created and committed as fbdfe7c; boot verification and Slice 1 implementation are now in flight.

Barry Bot: app now boots successfully, build/typecheck/lint pass, and live Prisma-backed operator dashboard, inbox, property/unit detail, status transitions, and internal notes are working in commit 0dde178.
Barry Bot: operator create/edit flows for properties, units, and requests are now live in commit fa83e96, bringing Slice 1 to formal Jeff QA review.
Barry Bot: Jeff fixed request validation, status-history integrity, and operator page revalidation issues in commit 0ce1e6b, clearing Slice 1 for operator scope.
Barry Bot: tenant submission, tenant-facing status/timeline, and photo upload landed in commit 58d3d98.
Barry Bot: Bob the Builder shipped vendor dispatch plus vendor queue/detail workflow in commit 25a92ea, making the multi-role flow materially real.
Barry Bot: next step is Jeff QA on the new vendor/tenant slices, then final polish and review-ready completion.
Barry Bot: Bob the Builder has been designated as Elon's focused and precise assistant coder for active implementation work.
Barry Bot: credential-based auth landed in commit 6230b65, replacing the demo role picker with real sign-in and canonical operator/tenant/vendor roles.
Barry Bot: stronger auth failure handling and vendor request permissions landed in commit a83f5be, including dedicated unauthorized routing and server-enforced vendor updates.
Barry Bot: invalid-session 500s were fixed in commit 46221bf by removing cookie mutation from render-time auth checks and preserving fail-closed redirects.

Barry Bot: Jeff cleared the auth/authz slice as PASS WITH CONDITIONS for a controlled private beta after confirming protected routes, ownership scoping, visibility boundaries, and invalid-session redirects are materially correct.

Barry Bot: auth boundary integration tests landed in commit 022bb72 covering tampered cookies, expired sessions, tenant object-access denial, vendor unassigned-request denial, and internal-note non-leakage.
Barry Bot: sellability path is now documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_SELLABILITY_CHECKLIST.md with explicit stages for pilot-ready, paid beta-ready, and publicly sellable.
Barry Bot: Property Manager is Chrome-compatible and Windows-compatible as a deployed web app, but it is not a native Windows application or a Chrome extension.

Barry Bot: PostgreSQL + pilot deployment planning landed in PROPERTY_MANAGER_POSTGRES_DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md via commit 5493a36, with Vercel + Neon + private Cloudflare R2 recommended as the simplest sane pilot stack.
Barry Bot: Bob hardened operator organization scoping in commit 8cb7e16 so operator reads, writes, and request actions no longer trust raw IDs across org boundaries.
Barry Bot: next quality gate on this slice is Jeff verifying foreign-org access denial and then cleaning up the auth-boundary test harness mismatch on valid-session automated cases.

Barry Bot: rollout direction is now explicit: the operator pays, tenant access is free, vendor access is free, and tenant/vendor accounts should join the operator's workspace via operator-generated invite links or access codes rather than acting like standalone paid products.

Barry Bot: product direction now includes optional regions inside an operator organization so properties and vendors can be grouped across multiple towns/service areas without prematurely turning that into a giant enterprise hierarchy.
Barry Bot: request workflow direction now explicitly includes visible urgency levels (Low / Medium / High / Emergency) and a separate expected completion date that can be updated as work evolves.

Barry Bot: CSV import/export is now part of product direction for setup and operational data, while full ticket history should be retained and 12-month reporting should be the default lens rather than a hard retention limit.
Mario: the right wedge is a maintenance command center, not an all-in-one property management suite.

apps.name: Clipboard Workflow Utility
apps.summary: Role-specific clipboard workflow utility for prosumers; now reframed as Windows-first, with Chrome later as a secondary surface.
apps.reviewGate: Jeff final QA review required before sign-off / ship.
apps.nextStep: Windows-first M1: WPF shell, text-only clipboard listener, SQLite persistence for last 100 items, recent history list, and click-to-copy loop.
notes: Barry Bot: kept active, but no longer framed as the top visible app track while realtor and property-manager work are being prioritized.

Elon: Windows-first plan uses a WPF desktop shell with text-only clipboard listener, SQLite persistence, and a basic recent-history loop. Chrome should be a later phase, not the primary product surface.
